pageDescription: A 10 ft × 10 ft area at 3 inches deep needs about 0.93 cubic yards or 1.4 US tons with 10% waste. Enter your dimensions below for instant cubic yards and tons.
densityHint: Standard crushed stone is about 1.5 tons per cubic yard. Adjust for your supplier's material.
importantBody: Enter depth as the thickness of stone you want to install (e.g. 4 inches for a driveway base). All length and width values must use the same unit you select above.
emptyHint: Choose a shape, enter dimensions and depth, then click Calculate material.
proTip1: Order 5–10% extra for compaction, spillage, and uneven subgrade.
proTip2: Driveway bases are often 4–6 inches deep; walkways 2–3 inches.
proTip3: Compact stone in lifts for driveways and shed pads for a stable base.
proTip4: Confirm tons per cubic yard with your stone yard — density varies by material and moisture.
proTip5: Divide large projects into sections and add volumes together for irregular layouts.
errDensity: Enter a valid density (0.5–3 tons per cubic yard).
